context: Nephrocalcinosis =  diffuse fine radiologically demonstrable calcifications deposited as calcium salts in renal parenchyma DDx:   Nephrolithiasis (= "kidney stones" = renal calculi = radiopaque / nonradiopaque hard crystalline minerals formed within lumen of the urinary tract (renal collecting system, ureter, bladder = urolithiasis) nephros, Greek = kidney; lithos, Greek = stone; calx, Latin = lime; calculus, Latin = pebble; osis, Greek = abnormal condition = "full of" Prevalence:   0.1-6%; M > F Cause: Hyperoxaluria, Hypercalcemia, Hypercalciuria
